name: Total Community
description_short: Total Community Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured financial institution serving Taylor and surrounding communities since 1959, offering loans, savings accounts, and banking services.
description_long:
Total Community Credit Union (TCCU) was established in 1959 and has spent over six decades serving its home city of Taylor and surrounding communities. As a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union, TCCU operates under the regulatory oversight of the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), a U.S. Government Agency that provides federal insurance on member accounts. The organization is committed to a member-first philosophy and maintains a dedicated team of part-time and full-time employees.

TCCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products and services designed to meet diverse member needs. These include checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with rates as high as 4.0% APY, auto loans starting at 4.49% APR, mortgage loans with their SmartDrop product allowing rate reductions post-closing, home equity lines of credit at 3.99% APR, personal loans, recreational vehicle loans, student loans, and VISA credit cards. Additionally, TCCU provides digital banking tools including online banking, mobile banking with check deposit capabilities, bill pay, and a financial coaching app called "My Finance Coach" designed to guide members through 10 financial themes. The credit union also offers youth-focused products like a Kids Club savings program and scholarships for member families.

TCCU distinguishes itself through several member-centric features. The SmartDrop Mortgage product is a notable innovation allowing members to benefit from rate decreases after closing. The credit union provides member perks and rewards through partnerships with brands like Trust & Will and TruStage. Member testimonials highlight personalized service, with examples of staff members like Amy M. actively helping members refinance vehicles at better rates. The organization maintains a scam awareness program and offers financial education through their My Finance Coach app, demonstrating commitment to member protection and financial literacy.

As a credit union, TCCU benefits from the cooperative model which typically results in competitive rates and member-owned governance. However, membership eligibility may be restricted to specific geographic communities or other criteria not fully detailed on the website. The digital banking infrastructure appears well-developed, though details on specific membership requirements, eligibility, and any membership fees are not prominently displayed on the available website content. For consumers in the Taylor area or those who qualify for membership, TCCU presents a traditional credit union option with broad product offerings and personalized service.
services: Checking and savings accounts with NCUA federal insurance
Certificates of Deposit (CD) up to 4.0% APY
Mortgage loans with SmartDrop rate reduction feature
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOC) at 3.99% APR
Mobile banking with mobile check deposit
My Finance Coach financial education app
pros: NCUA federal insurance on all member deposits, providing full account protection
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.49% APR
SmartDrop Mortgage product allows rate reductions if market rates drop after closing
4.0% APY on Certificates of Deposit with only $500 minimum balance
My Finance Coach app provides personalized financial education and guidance
Mobile banking with mobile check deposit capability for convenience
Member perks and rewards through partnerships with established brands like Trust & Will
Member-owned cooperative structure eliminates profit-driven pricing incentives
cons: Membership eligibility criteria not clearly stated on website, may be geographically restricted
Limited information about membership fees, minimum balance requirements, or account maintenance costs
No mention of overdraft protection options or specific fee structure for common services
Customer service chat availability is limited (Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-6pm)
HELOC and mortgage rates shown are variable or promotional, actual rates depend on credit approval
best_for: Residents of Taylor and surrounding communities seeking personalized local credit union service
Members interested in mortgages with rate-reduction flexibility through SmartDrop features
Savers looking for competitive CD rates (4.0% APY) with low minimum deposits
Families wanting youth financial education through Kids Club and scholarship programs
diagnosis: Total Community Credit Union is best suited for residents of the Taylor area and surrounding communities who qualify for membership and value personalized, local credit union service with competitive rates and member-owned governance. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted geographically or by other criteria not fully explained on the website, so prospective members should verify they qualify before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
